rooms.name: FASTROOM KOENJI
rooms.station: Koenji (JR Chuo/Sobu) ~12 min walk or 5 min bus; Nakano ~3 min bus
rooms.fees: +¥10,000–16,000 maintenance/mo (utilities + WiFi)
rooms.oneTime: Contract fee ¥20,000–50,000 (one-time)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — monthly lease, short-term OK; bookable from abroad via Oakhouse
rooms.contact: Oakhouse online inquiry + English support
rooms.note: Wooden 2-story house on a Koenji shopping street with cafes, gyms and a public bath — right in the live-music/indie belt. Single + semi-private rooms.

rooms.name: OAK TERRACE SANGENJAYA
rooms.station: Sangenjaya (Tokyu Setagaya/Den-en-toshi) ~15 min walk; Shibuya ~5 min by train
rooms.fees: +¥16,000 maintenance/mo (utilities + optical internet)
rooms.oneTime: Contract fee ¥20,000–50,000 (one-time)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — monthly lease, short-term OK; bookable from abroad
rooms.contact: Oakhouse online inquiry + English support
rooms.note: 5-building complex walkable to both Shimokitazawa and Sangenjaya, with rooftop garden — prime indie/live-house Setagaya. Single rooms (Bldg A) + 2LDK shared apartments (Bldg B–E). Non-smoking.

rooms.name: SA073 TOKYO β Koenji 1 (formerly SA-Xross Koenji 2)
rooms.station: Koenji (JR Chuo/Sobu) — Shinjuku ~10 min by train
rooms.fees: +~¥15,000 common service fee/mo (water/electric/gas/WiFi) + ¥1,500 system fee
rooms.oneTime: Registration fee ¥30,000 (non-refundable; periodic 50%-off campaigns)
rooms.moveIn: 1-year contract but stay as short as ~1 month with notice; book from abroad, intl. money transfer for initial costs
rooms.contact: XROSS HOUSE EN site / tel 03-6712-4346; English + 10+ languages
rooms.note: Budget private room steps from Koenji's shopping streets and live houses. ¥0 deposit/key money, flat low initial cost — verify the registration-fee campaign at booking.

rooms.name: SA119 TOKYO β Nogata 15 (formerly SA-Xross Koenji 3)
rooms.station: Nogata (Seibu Shinjuku) ~12 min walk; Koenji (JR) ~16 min walk
rooms.fees: +¥15,000 common service fee/mo (water/electric/gas/WiFi) + ¥1,500 system fee — ~¥64,500/mo all-in
rooms.oneTime: Registration fee ¥30,000 (non-refundable); move-out fee ¥15,000
rooms.moveIn: 1-year fixed term, early exit with ~1-month notice; book from abroad, intl. money transfer accepted
rooms.contact: XROSS HOUSE EN site; English, Korean, Chinese, Vietnamese, etc.
rooms.note: Small 14-person house with a known all-in monthly figure (~¥64,500) and ¥0 deposit/key money — easy to budget. Between Koenji and Nogata.

rooms.name: N3 XROSS Nakano Sakaue 1
rooms.station: Nakano-sakaue (Toei Oedo/Marunouchi) ~10 min walk; Okubo (JR) ~9 min walk
rooms.fees: +¥15,000 common service fee/mo (water/electric/gas/WiFi) + ¥1,500 system fee — ~¥44,500/mo all-in
rooms.oneTime: Registration fee ¥30,000 (non-refundable); move-out fee ¥15,000
rooms.moveIn: 1-year term but stay from ~1 month with notice; book from abroad, intl. money transfer accepted
rooms.contact: XROSS HOUSE EN site; English + multiple languages
rooms.note: Cheapest landing pad on this list — 13-bed dorm, ~¥44,500/mo all-in, one stop from Nakano. Good first-month base while you house-hunt; verify a dorm bed is free.

rooms.name: Come on UP Shimokitazawa
rooms.station: Shimokitazawa ~8 min walk; Higashi-kitazawa ~6 min walk
rooms.fees: Utilities/WiFi — confirm at inquiry (high-speed WiFi included)
rooms.oneTime: Confirm initial fees with operator
rooms.moveIn: Min 12-month contract; small house, often waitlisted — apply early via Tokyo Sharehouse
rooms.contact: Listed on Tokyo Sharehouse (Come on UP Ltd); English support
rooms.note: 7-room house ~8 min from Shimokitazawa — the live-music/indie/vintage heart of Tokyo. Deliberately ~50% Japanese / ~50% foreign, English+Japanese spoken. Popular, so it's often full — verify openings.

rooms.name: Come on UP Todoroki
rooms.station: Kuhonbutsu (Tokyu Oimachi) ~7 min walk; Oyamadai ~7 min walk
rooms.fees: Utilities — confirm at inquiry
rooms.oneTime: Confirm initial fees with operator
rooms.moveIn: Book via Tokyo Sharehouse; small 9-room house, can be full — apply early
rooms.contact: Listed on Tokyo Sharehouse (Come on UP Ltd); English site
rooms.note: Quiet, leafy 9LDK Setagaya house with a tight international community (monthly house dinners). Refundable deposit. Calmer than Shimokita but easy Oimachi-line access to it and Shibuya.

rooms.name: SAKURA HOUSE HIGASHI KOENJI
rooms.station: Higashi-koenji (Tokyo Metro Marunouchi) ~15 min walk
rooms.rent: Share house from ~¥75,000 / mo (opening discount up to ¥7,000/mo off for first 3 months)
rooms.fees: Utilities + WiFi + furniture included in rent
rooms.oneTime: No key money; no long contract required — confirm move-in/admin fee
rooms.moveIn: Book online before arrival; flexible monthly stays, no long lease required
rooms.contact: Sakura House online booking; English support (operator since 1992)
rooms.note: Newer Sakura House building near Myohoji temple and Sanshi-no-mori park, one Marunouchi stop from Koenji's live-music scene. Apartment + share-house rooms, all-inclusive rent, fully bookable from abroad. Verify the opening-discount and current rooms.

rooms.name: Borderless House Roppongi1 (Nishi-Azabu)
rooms.station: Roppongi / Hiroo — walk to Aoyama-Itchome; quick to Shibuya, Ginza, Shinjuku
rooms.fees: Utilities + internet bundled (typical ~¥10,000–15,000/mo at Borderless houses)
rooms.oneTime: ~¥30,000 key money + cleaning (varies by house)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — book from abroad; this specific house often full, waitlist available
rooms.contact: Online inquiry, English site + staff
rooms.note: A SPECIFIC Borderless house (16 beds: 14 private + 1 shared), 100% residents in their 20s, enforced 50:50 Japanese:foreign ratio. LANGUAGE EXCHANGE + Borderless Diners community programs; pricey-area Minato address. Verify current vacancy.

rooms.name: Borderless House Komagome
rooms.station: Komagome — JR Yamanote + Metro Namboku (6 min walk); Ikebukuro 7 min, Shinjuku 16 min, Shibuya 22 min
rooms.fees: ¥15,000/mo utilities + internet
rooms.oneTime: ¥45,000 initial (¥30,000 key money + ¥15,000 cleaning); optional ¥12,000 bedding
rooms.deposit: Folded into initial fee (no large deposit)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — book from abroad; often full with waitlist
rooms.contact: Online inquiry, English site + staff
rooms.note: Specific Yamanote-loop Borderless house: ~10–14 residents, 7 countries / 11 nationalities (NZ, Spain, China etc.); many housemates use English daily. 50:50 local:foreign mix. Private rooms 11.7–20㎡. Verify current vacancy.

rooms.name: Borderless House Ikebukuro-Oyama1
rooms.station: Oyama — Tobu Tojo Line (1 min walk); 5 min to Ikebukuro, 13 min to Takadanobaba
rooms.fees: ¥15,000/mo utilities + internet
rooms.oneTime: ¥45,000 initial (¥30,000 key money + ¥15,000 cleaning); optional ¥12,000 bedding
rooms.deposit: Folded into initial fee (no large deposit)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — book from abroad
rooms.contact: Online inquiry, English site + staff
rooms.note: Specific Borderless house: all 17 rooms private, 8 countries / 17 tenants, 20s–30s. Rooms come furnished (bed, desk, fridge, AC). 1-min walk to station, fast Ikebukuro access. Many Japanese housemates keen on language exchange. Verify current vacancy.

rooms.name: World Neighbors Gokokuji (Social Apartment)
rooms.station: Gokokuji — Metro Yurakucho (8 min); Shin-Otsuka — Metro Marunouchi (8 min); JR Otsuka 15 min
rooms.rent: ¥77,000–79,000 / mo (avg ~¥54k across Social Apt portfolio; this building higher-end)
rooms.fees: Common-service fee + utilities on top (verify per building)
rooms.oneTime: Low initial vs standard leases (Social Apt model)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — apply online
rooms.contact: Online application, English site
rooms.note: Specific large design building (~180 units) with a ground-floor café, big lounge, workspaces, theater room, fitness room, bar lounge and rooftop balcony — the flagship 'design + community' format. International mix, but skews Japanese-resident-majority vs Borderless. Verify current rent/availability.

rooms.name: Neighbors Shibuya Kamiyamacho (Social Apartment)
rooms.station: Shibuya (10 min walk); Yoyogi-Park (8 min walk)
rooms.fees: Common-service fee + utilities on top (verify per building)
rooms.oneTime: Low initial vs standard leases (Social Apt model)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — apply online (recent batches available mid-July onward)
rooms.contact: Online application, English site
rooms.note: Flagship design building in trendy Oku-Shibuya: standout theater room, working space, bar lounge. Premium Shibuya-walk pricing. Mixed but Japanese-resident-leaning community; strong design/social-space focus. Verify current rent/availability.

rooms.name: SHARE HOUSE 180° — community houses (Kamiitabashi etc.)
rooms.area: Citywide Tokyo (~142 Tokyo houses; e.g. Kamiitabashi in Itabashi)
rooms.station: Various — e.g. Kamiitabashi (Tobu Tojo Line)
rooms.fees: Separate common-area + utilities fee per house
rooms.oneTime: Small reservation fee; no key money / agent fee (share-house model)
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — apply online
rooms.contact: English site + online inquiry
rooms.note: Community-first operator ('change from encounters'): in-house community APP for self-intros, events, and resident-run clubs (drinks/sports/manga), connecting across its Tokyo/Kanto/Nagoya/Sapporo houses. International + Japanese mix; explicit women-only houses with a younger resident base; very affordable budget rooms. Verify per-house current rent/availability.

rooms.name: Stay and Tokyo (TOKYOSTAY) — Otsuka-area share houses & apartments, all-in rent
rooms.station: Within ~5 min walk of stations (Otsuka on Yamanote, Gokokuji, Shiinamachi on Seibu Ikebukuro, Sugamo)
rooms.fees: All utilities + internet included in rent
rooms.oneTime: Zero key money, zero agent fees
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — online reservation, bookable from abroad ('start with just a suitcase', furnished + linens)
rooms.contact: Multilingual site (EN/中文/FR/IT/KR/ES); info@tokyostay.co.jp, 03-5980-9621
rooms.note: Operating since 2010, guests from 50+ countries; international + Japanese-student mix, clustered around Otsuka (Yamanote line, quick to Ikebukuro/Shinjuku). Flat all-inclusive monthly price (utilities + Wi-Fi), no guarantor, no key money, furnished — a clean soft-landing into a real neighborhood. Confirm exact deposit and minimum stay.

rooms.name: XROSS HOUSE — budget no-guarantor share houses & furnished apartments
rooms.area: Citywide — Tokyo 23 wards (Nakano, Itabashi, Nerima, Adachi, Katsushika cheapest) + Mitaka, Musashino, Machida, Hachioji, Tachikawa, Fuchu
rooms.station: Various (200+ houses)
rooms.fees: Common/utility fee per house (low; Wi-Fi typically included)
rooms.oneTime: ¥0 deposit, ¥0 key money, ¥0 brokerage
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — full online/web contract by smartphone, signable from abroad; 360° virtual viewing; min stay ~1 month
rooms.contact: 11-language support incl. English; online contract
rooms.note: One of the cheapest legit foreigner-oriented operators (private/semi-private/dorm rooms): rooms from ~¥25k in outer wards (20–40 min to central), ¥0 deposit/key money/brokerage, online lease signing from overseas, and free moves between its 200+ properties anytime. Strong multilingual infrastructure; community is functional rather than design-led (lower polish than Social Apartment). Outer-ward cheap rooms trade central location for price — verify current rent/availability.

rooms.name: GG House — cheap private rooms, young crowd
rooms.area: Citywide — Yamanote (Ikebukuro, Shinjuku, Shibuya, Ebisu, Shinagawa) + Chuo/Sobu line; cheapest in Adachi
rooms.station: Various (750+ properties)
rooms.fees: Utilities included; weekly cleaning
rooms.oneTime: First-month campaigns advertised (e.g. ~¥5,000 first month on 3+ month stays) — verify current terms
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — bookable from abroad, multilingual contract
rooms.contact: Online inquiry, support in English + 7 other languages
rooms.note: Big budget operator (30,000+ guests from 100+ countries). All-private rooms, utilities + weekly cleaning included, women-only rooms available. Cheapest rooms (~¥28k) are in outer wards like Adachi. Reported 18–33 age skew — confirm before booking.

rooms.name: Modern Living Japan — central no-deposit share
rooms.area: Central Tokyo (Shinjuku, Shibuya, Roppongi, Minato, Meguro, Ikebukuro)
rooms.station: Various
rooms.fees: Verify what utilities are included
rooms.oneTime: No deposit, no guarantor; promo ¥35,000 off first 90 days advertised — verify
rooms.moveIn: Rolling — WhatsApp/LINE/phone inquiry, bookable from abroad
requirements: No guarantor, no deposit, no agency fee (advertised)
rooms.contact: English support; WhatsApp/LINE/phone +81-3-3268-2411
rooms.note: Positions itself as cheap central housing ('from ¥40k, no deposit, no guarantor'). More central than XROSS/GG cheapest rooms. Verify the move-in promo and exactly what's bundled into rent before signing.

rooms.name: Interwhao — no deposit/key-money share houses
rooms.area: Tokyo (Kinshicho, Futako-Tamagawa, Shin-Otsuka, Jiyugaoka) + Yokohama, Kawasaki, Saitama, Chiba
rooms.station: Various
rooms.fees: Some houses include internet; regular cleaning
rooms.oneTime: Several houses: no deposit, no guarantor, no key money
rooms.deposit: ¥0 on many houses — verify per listing
rooms.moveIn: Inquiry online; some houses prefer 6+ month stays
rooms.contact: English site; phone 0120-929-959 (9–19), email inquiry
rooms.note: Budget operator with several 'no deposit/guarantor/key money' houses from ~¥31.5k. Some properties give preference to 6+ month tenants — good if you want stability, less so for a short soft-landing. Verify each house's terms individually.

rooms.name: J&F Plaza (J&F House) — cheap, but guarantor-co may apply
rooms.station: Various (J&F House Shinjuku, Musashi-Urawa, Warabi, etc.)
rooms.fees: Fixed utilities required on top of rent; free internet (bring own LAN cable)
rooms.oneTime: ~¥30,000 non-refundable entrance fee + first month; deposit/guarantor-company fee on some houses
rooms.moveIn: Min stay 1 month; fully furnished
requirements: A guarantor COMPANY may be required on some houses (not strictly no-guarantor)
rooms.contact: English support; 03-6455-0360, contact form
rooms.note: Long-running operator (~50% foreign residents, 100+ countries) with very cheap rooms from ~¥20k. Caveat: their own FAQ says a specified guarantor COMPANY and a deposit may be required on some houses — so unlike XROSS/GG it is not blanket no-guarantor. Confirm the specific house's terms.

rooms.name: Arden (Share Style) — curated / women-only houses
rooms.station: Various
rooms.fees: Flat fee — internet, gas, electricity, water all included
rooms.oneTime: Verify deposit/initial cost per house
rooms.moveIn: Inquiry online; English-speaking staff
rooms.contact: English-speaking staff; online contact form
rooms.note: Smaller, curated houses (low headcount) with strong language-exchange / community programming and monthly house parties; several women-only properties. Flat all-inclusive utilities. Confirm guarantor/deposit and women-only availability on the specific house, since terms aren't published site-wide.

rooms.name: Sakura Hotel & Hostel — monthly plan (soft landing)
rooms.area: Central Tokyo — Jimbocho, Hatagaya, Ikebukuro, Asakusa, Nippori
rooms.station: Jimbocho (2 min walk) + 4 other branches
rooms.rent: Discounted monthly rate — request quote (nightly dorm ~¥3,000+, private higher)
rooms.fees: Free Wi-Fi in room; breakfast at Sakura Cafe included
rooms.oneTime: None (hotel booking, not a lease)
rooms.gender: mixed (dorms + private; women-only dorm at some branches)
rooms.moveIn: Same-day possible; book online from abroad
requirements: No address registration needed for short stay
rooms.contact: English-speaking staff (24h front desk); online inquiry form
rooms.note: Classic week-1 soft landing: well-rated (8.9/10), English staff, 24h desk, laundry, central. Offers a discounted monthly extended-stay plan — get a quote via the inquiry form (rate not published). Use it to land, sort a SIM/bank/address, then move into a share house.

rooms.name: UNPLAN Kagurazaka — design hostel (soft landing)
rooms.station: Kagurazaka (<5 min walk)
rooms.rent: Nightly from ~¥5,000 (~$33); ask about weekly/long-stay rate
rooms.fees: Free Wi-Fi; in-room washer/dryer + microwave; on-site cafe
rooms.oneTime: None (hotel booking)
rooms.moveIn: Book online from abroad; same-day possible
rooms.contact: English-speaking staff; book via own site or Hostelworld/Booking
rooms.note: Stylish, sociable soft-landing hostel in a great central neighborhood, with in-room laundry (handy for a multi-week stay) and a cafe to work from. No published monthly plan — ask for a long-stay/weekly rate, or just book nightly while you secure a share house. Verify current rates at booking.

rooms.name: UR danchi — public rental (no key money / no guarantor / no agent fee)
rooms.area: Citywide Kanto (Tokyo 23-ward danchi + Kawasaki, Yokohama, Saitama, Chiba)
rooms.station: Various — many danchi are a bus ride from the nearest station
rooms.fees: Utilities billed separately; no renewal fee, no agent fee, no guarantor-company fee
rooms.oneTime: No key money, no agent fee — only deposit
rooms.moveIn: After arrival only — needs a residence card in hand, so not bookable from abroad
requirements: Residence card (issued on arrival at the airport for WHV)
No guarantor and no guarantor company required
Income roughly 4x monthly rent annually, or savings/lump-sum-rent alternative
Apply in person or online at a UR center
rooms.contact: UR rental centers; English-speaking agents via partners (e.g. ur-housing.com / WhiteStone)
rooms.note: The classic low-upfront route: UR formally waives key money, guarantor, agent commission AND renewal fees on all units — a national policy, not case-by-case. Trade-off: stock is mostly older 1970s–90s buildings and you must already hold a residence card, so line this up for month 1–2 after landing, not before. The income/savings test is the main gate for a new WHV arrival; UR accepts a lump-sum rent prepayment as a savings alternative.

rooms.name: Village House — budget apartments (no key money / no deposit / no fees)
rooms.area: Nationwide incl. greater Tokyo / Kanto (often outer wards & suburbs)
rooms.station: Various — many estates are suburban, bus-or-bike from the station
rooms.rent: Tokyo 1R from ~¥100,000 / mo; nationwide rents start ~¥20,000 / mo
rooms.fees: No renewal fee, no handling fee; utilities separate
rooms.oneTime: No key money, no agency fee
rooms.deposit: Usually none — but a deposit may apply depending on contract/screening
rooms.moveIn: Rolling; English call centre (0120-266-078) and email intake
requirements: Often no guarantor / no guarantor company — but a joint guarantor can be requested depending on screening
Foreign residents accepted on all properties
rooms.contact: English support via call centre + email; 'gaijin-friendly' branding
rooms.note: Lowest-possible upfront cost: explicitly no key money, no deposit (usually), no renewal/handling fee, all units open to foreigners. The catch is location — the cheapest stock is suburban/outer-Kanto, and Tokyo-proper 1R starts nearer ¥100k. Guarantor is frequently waived but not guaranteed; budget for a guarantor-company fee in case screening asks for one. Verify current Tokyo availability and the exact deposit/guarantor terms on the unit before committing.

rooms.name: MetroResidences — furnished serviced apartments (no guarantor)
rooms.area: Central Tokyo (Minato, Shibuya, Shinjuku and surrounding wards)
rooms.station: Various central-line stations
rooms.fees: Internet + utilities included (with cap); cleaning fee
rooms.oneTime: No key money, no agent fee
rooms.deposit: One refundable month's deposit + cleaning fee (~half the initial cost vs a normal lease)
rooms.moveIn: Bookable from abroad (online / email booking.jp@metroresidences.com); 30-day notice to leave
requirements: Valid ID / passport (requirements vary by unit)
rooms.contact: English online booking + phone (03-4405-5226), 24/7 enquiries
rooms.note: A polished from-abroad soft-landing into a real private apartment (not a share house): furnished, utilities/internet bundled, no guarantor, and you can sign before you fly. Price is the trade-off — it sits at the splurge end (¥180k+), so it suits the first 1–6 months while you set up a residence card and bank account, then move to UR/Village House for cheaper long-term rent. Longer stays unlock a half-month-free-per-6-months discount. Verify current rates and availability — pricing scales with lease length.

rooms.name: Leopalace21 — monthly furnished mansions (no guarantor)
rooms.station: Various
rooms.fees: Many units include free water + utilities; internet often included
rooms.oneTime: No agency fee; key money/deposit waived on many units (filterable)
rooms.deposit: Varies — filter for 'no security deposit / no key money' units
rooms.moveIn: Rolling; online consultation + e-contract, English site
rooms.contact: English/Chinese/Korean website with online consultation & contract
rooms.note: The big-network monthly option: furnished studios with appliances, free utilities on many units, no guarantor, and screening that weighs ability-to-pay over Japanese ability — genuinely foreigner-easy. Reputation caveat: Leopalace is known for thin walls and variable build quality, so read recent unit reviews. Monthly-plan rates run higher per month than a standard year lease; good as a flexible 1–6 month base. Verify the per-unit key-money/deposit and current monthly rate, which fluctuate.

rooms.name: Apartment Japan — book a private apartment from abroad (no guarantor, no JP bank)
rooms.station: Various
rooms.fees: Many units state no agency fee; initial costs shown transparently before booking
rooms.oneTime: Many properties explicitly no key money, no agency fee
rooms.deposit: Varies by unit — displayed up front
rooms.moveIn: Fully remote — search, quote, apply and e-sign (DocuSign) before arrival; min 1 month
requirements: No Japanese guarantor / no guarantee company enrolment
No Japanese bank account — uses your home-country credit card as security
rooms.contact: 24/7 English support; entire process online incl. e-signature
rooms.note: The cleanest sign-before-you-fly route into a private apartment: no guarantor, no Japanese bank account, and the lease is e-signed via DocuSign from Canada using a home-country credit card as the security mechanism. Furnished options exist (rare in Japan). Premium over local-market rent is the cost of that convenience. Ideal for locking housing before you land, then optionally switching to UR/Village House once you have a residence card and bank account. Verify the per-unit initial cost and current availability before booking.

rooms.name: AtHearth — foreigner-focused agency (guarantor-company route, apply from abroad)
rooms.station: Various
rooms.rent: Market rent; examples ~¥172,000–390,000 / mo (full catalogue runs lower for studios)
rooms.fees: Standard agency move-in costs; optional paid concierge add-ons
rooms.oneTime: Filter of 2,400+ 'no key money' listings; guarantor-company fee applies instead of a personal guarantor
rooms.moveIn: Apply from abroad via their platform; start ~2–3 months out, viewings ~3 weeks out, sign 1–2 weeks before
requirements: No personal Japanese guarantor — a guarantor company (hoshō gaisha) is arranged instead
rooms.contact: English digital platform + agents; 10+ years serving expats
rooms.note: An English-first brokerage rather than a single landlord: it sources normal market apartments and clears the two foreigner blockers for you — it arranges a guarantor company (hoshō gaisha) so you need no personal Japanese guarantor, and has a large 'no key money' filter. You can start the process from Canada. Because these are regular market leases, expect a standard guarantor-company fee (~¥30,000–60,000 + annual renewal) and normal move-in costs; this buys you a long-term unbranded apartment rather than a serviced one. Verify the specific unit's key-money/deposit and the guarantor-company fee before signing.

rooms.name: Wagaya Japan — multilingual no-guarantor apartment portal
rooms.station: Various
rooms.fees: Maintenance fee ~¥15,000/mo on many units; utilities separate
rooms.oneTime: Many listings show key money ¥0 (deposit/key shown as 0mo/0mo)
rooms.moveIn: Multilingual 'request a property search' intake; English interface for remote enquiry
requirements: Multilingual: EN / 简体 / 繁體 / Tiếng Việt / 日本語
rooms.contact: Multilingual listing portal with property-search request form
rooms.note: A listing portal (not a landlord) with a built-in 'no guarantor' filter and very low advertised rents — some studios show ¥0 deposit / ¥0 key money. Watch the headline numbers: the cheapest figures often carry a ~¥15,000/mo maintenance fee and tiny units, and remote-application terms aren't spelled out, so confirm per-unit. Strong multilingual support makes it an easy place to short-list no-guarantor units. Verify the unit's true all-in monthly cost (rent + maintenance), deposit, and whether they'll process a from-abroad application.

events.title: Oedo Antique Market — Tokyo International Forum
events.area: Yurakucho / Marunouchi (Tokyo International Forum open plaza)
events.cost: Free entry; bring cash for vendors
events.bookingNotes: Japan's largest outdoor antique market, ~250 dealers in the Forum's open plaza. RECURRING: roughly the 1st and 3rd Sunday of most months (dates drift month-to-month and it does NOT run in August), 9:00–16:00, weather permitting. The 2026-07-05 date is the next likely session after arrival — VERIFY the exact monthly dates at antique-market.jp/english before going. Easy first-weekend outing 5 min from Yurakucho/Tokyo stations.
events.why: A reliable, free, recurring weekend anchor for vintage/synth/camera/ceramics hunting right in central Tokyo — perfect low-commitment social outing soon after landing.

events.title: Tomioka Hachimangu Antique & Flea Market (Monzen-Nakacho)
events.bookingNotes: Antique market on the shrine grounds, ~50–120 stalls (Imari ceramics, lacquerware, vintage kimono, old tools). RECURRING: antique market on the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th Sundays; separate flea market typically on the 15th and 28th. 08:00–dusk, CANCELLED in rain. 2026-07-12 is an illustrative Sunday — verify the month's schedule before going. 3 min from Monzen-Nakacho Station.
events.why: A deeper, less touristy antiques hunt than Oedo, on atmospheric shrine grounds — good recurring weekend ritual for collectors.

events.title: Setagaya Boro-ichi — historic 440-year flea market
events.area: Setagaya (Boroichi-dori, between Setagaya & Kamimachi Stations, Tokyu Setagaya Line)
events.cost: Free entry; cash for vendors and street food
events.bookingNotes: Running since 1578 and a designated Tokyo Intangible Folk Cultural Property: 700+ vendors, ~200,000 visitors, used goods/antiques/handicrafts/daikan-mochi rice cakes. FIXED twice-yearly dates: December 15–16 and January 15–16 every year (so also 2027-01-15/16). 09:00–20:00, free. Get off at Setagaya Station, walk Boroichi-dori, exit via Kamimachi.
events.why: Tokyo's most historic flea market and a genuine cultural event, not a tourist trap — a must-do winter outing with reliable fixed dates you can plan around.

events.title: Farmer's Market @ UNU (Aoyama / Omotesando)
events.area: Aoyama / Omotesando (plaza in front of United Nations University)
events.cost: Free entry; pay vendors (produce, natural wine, street food)
events.bookingNotes: One of Tokyo's longest-running farmers markets: ~20+ farmers plus natural-wine and fermented-food stalls, food trucks, and frequent themed mini-events. RECURRING: every Saturday AND Sunday, 10:00–16:00, ~5 min from Omotesando Station exit B2. 2026-07-04 is just the first weekend after arrival — it runs essentially every weekend year-round.
events.why: A dependable, free, every-weekend social/food hub in a central neighborhood — ideal default spot to meet people, graze, and decompress on arrival weekends.

events.title: VENT Omotesando — weekend techno/house club nights
events.cost: Door ~¥2,000–3,500 depending on lineup; cash + ID (20+) required
events.bookingNotes: Tokyo's leading minimal/deep-house & techno room with a custom sound system and regular international guest DJs. RECURRING: open Fri/Sat/Sun, ~23:00–05:00. 2026-07-03 is the first Friday after arrival — check the rolling lineup at vent-tokyo.net (or RA/Discotech) for who's playing. Bring passport; no entry without photo ID.
events.why: The most consistent serious-techno club in the city and an easy recurring night out for the electronic-music pillar — quality lineups nearly every weekend.

events.title: WOMB Shibuya — techno/house club nights
events.cost: Door typically ~¥3,000–4,000 (more for headliners); cash + ID (20+)
events.bookingNotes: Four-floor Shibuya institution since 2000, Asia's largest mirror ball and a flagship sound system, world-class techno/house on the main floor. RECURRING: events most Fri/Sat plus special nights; ~23:00–05:00. 2026-07-04 is illustrative — check the calendar at womb.co.jp/en/event for headliners and advance tickets (often cheaper than door). Bring passport.
events.why: A bucket-list big-room club for the music pillar with a near-constant stream of international headliners — the counterpoint to Vent's intimate room.

events.title: Circus Tokyo — underground club nights (Shibuya)
events.bookingNotes: Intimate two-floor Shibuya venue (open since 2015, sister to Osaka's Circus) known for credible house/techno/bass bookings and a strong local-DJ community — a go-to since Contact closed in 2022. RECURRING: events most weekends, ~23:00–05:00. 2026-07-11 is illustrative — see Resident Advisor (ra.co) or the venue site for the rolling lineup. Bring passport.
events.why: A smaller, more underground room than Womb for discovering local talent and sweaty late-night sets — rounds out the electronic-music rotation.

events.title: Forestlimit (Hatagaya) — experimental / listening-bar nights
events.cost: Door ~¥1,500–2,500, often incl. a drink; cash
events.bookingNotes: Tiny experimental venue/listening bar with four giant speaker stacks; programming swings from noise and ambient to disco and bass via deep-listening residencies. RECURRING: Thursday is the regular deep-listening night, with curated parties most weekends (e.g. TOTEM TRAXX / DO SHOCK BOOZE pop-ups). 2026-07-09 is an illustrative Thursday — check forestlimit.com or RA for the actual schedule.
events.why: The opposite end of the spectrum from the big clubs — an intimate, adventurous space for left-field electronic and live experimental music, great for meeting the underground scene.

events.title: teamLab Borderless — Azabudai Hills (permanent digital art)
events.area: Azabudai Hills / Kamiyacho (Garden Plaza B, B1F; ~5 min from Kamiyacho Stn)
events.bookingNotes: Permanent immersive digital-art museum (reopened Azabudai Hills, Feb 2024) with borderless rooms — 'Bubble Universe', 'Megalith Crystal Formation', etc. OPEN year-round; this is NOT a single-day event — date shown as an arrival-window anchor. BOOK timed-entry tickets in advance on teamlab.art (often sell out weeks ahead, especially weekends). Distinct from teamLab Planets in Toyosu.
events.why: Tokyo's flagship immersive art experience and an easy rainy-day or visitor outing — book it once early when prices/slots are good rather than waiting.

events.title: Mariko Mori Retrospective — Mori Art Museum (Roppongi)
events.cost: ~¥2,000–2,500 adult (typical Mori Art Museum special-exhibition pricing); verify
events.bookingNotes: Major retrospective of Mariko Mori (art + science + metaphysics): ~80 works across three decades — interactive installations, sculpture, video, photography, performance. Runs Oct 31, 2026 → Mar 28, 2027 per the museum's announced FY2026 schedule. Open into the evening; ticket spans a long window so easy to fit in. Confirm exact price/hours on mori.art.museum closer to opening.
events.why: A landmark, long-running contemporary-art show you can catch anytime over five months — high-quality indoor culture for the winter, with city-view museum access included.

events.title: Art Week Tokyo 2026 (citywide galleries + free AWT Bus)
events.area: Citywide (55 venues: galleries, institutions, AWT Focus spaces)
events.cost: Many gallery shows free; some institutions ticketed; free hop-on/off AWT Bus
events.bookingNotes: 5th edition, Nov 4–8, 2026: a record 55 venues (32 galleries, 13 institutions, 10 AWT Focus spaces) linked by a free hop-on/hop-off AWT Bus (10:00–18:00, new express lines). Collaboration with Art Basel. Plan a route in advance via artweektokyo.com; the bus makes gallery-hopping across Roppongi/Tennozu/Ginza practical.
events.why: The single best window to plug into Tokyo's contemporary-art scene in one concentrated stretch — free transport between dozens of galleries makes it efficient and social.

events.title: Roppongi Art Night 2026 (all-night art festival)
events.area: Roppongi (Roppongi Hills, Mori Art Museum, Tokyo Midtown, streets)
events.cost: Mostly free outdoor/street program; some indoor venues ticketed
events.bookingNotes: All-night art festival returning for the first time in 3 years, Oct 31 – Nov 1, 2026, with art/music/video/performance scattered through Roppongi from dusk till dawn (2026 RAN Focus theme: France / Nuit Blanche). Overlaps the same weekend as the Mariko Mori opening and just before Art Week — you can stack all three. Crowded; plan a loose route.
events.why: A rare dusk-to-dawn art takeover of a whole district — a uniquely Tokyo night out that pairs perfectly with the late-night/club rhythm you're after.

events.title: Tokyo Comic Con 2026 (Makuhari Messe)
events.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba (Keiyo/Musashino Line to Kaihimmakuhari)
events.cost: Day tickets ~¥3,000–5,000+ (photo/autograph ops priced separately); buy advance
events.bookingNotes: US-style comic con fusing American + Japanese pop culture: movie props, celebrity guests, cosplay, exclusive merch. Dates Dec 11 (Fri) – Dec 13 (Sun), 2026 at Makuhari Messe. Buy tickets in advance on tokyocomiccon.jp; celebrity photo-ops and autographs sell out and are ticketed on top of admission. Note this is in Chiba, ~40–50 min from central Tokyo.
events.why: A different flavor from Comiket/TGS — Western-meets-Japanese fandom with guest celebrities and props, slotting neatly into the geek/anime pillar in mid-December.

events.title: Kawagoe Festival (Kawagoe Matsuri) — day trip
events.area: Kawagoe, Saitama ('Little Edo' Kurazukuri warehouse district)
events.cost: Free to watch; train ~¥500–700 each way + food
events.bookingNotes: UNESCO-listed float festival (Oct 17–18, 2026): ornate multi-tiered floats with dolls paraded through the historic warehouse district, climaxing in 'Hikkawase' float-vs-float musical face-offs after dark. DAY TRIP: ~30 min from Ikebukuro on the Tobu Tojo Line, or ~50 min from Shinjuku via JR. Draws ~1 million over two days — go early, expect heavy crowds; verify dates on kawagoematsuri.jp.
events.why: An accessible, spectacular traditional festival just outside Tokyo with a great old-town setting — a strong autumn day-trip that isn't another Tokyo fireworks show.

events.title: Ichigo-gari (strawberry picking) — winter day trip
events.area: Greenhouse farms in Chiba / Saitama / Tochigi (1–2 hr from Tokyo)
events.cost: ~¥2,000–3,000+ adult for 30–40 min all-you-can-eat (varies by date/farm)
events.bookingNotes: All-you-can-eat strawberry picking in heated greenhouses — peak sweetness is Jan–March. RECURRING SEASON, not a single date: 2027-01-16 is an illustrative weekend; the season runs ~late Dec → May. Farms cluster in Chiba, Saitama and Tochigi, most reachable by train as a day trip. RESERVE ahead (official site or a booking service) — popular farms and weekends fill up and may refuse walk-ins. Verify each farm's open dates and price.
events.why: A quintessential cozy winter day trip out of the city — cheap, social, indoors-warm, and easy to pair with an onsen or a small-town wander.
